ddls:
CREATE TABLE "weather_stations" (
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather station */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather station */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the weather station */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the weather station */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the weather station */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the weather station */,
  "station_type" TEXT /* Type of weather station */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the weather station collecting the data */,
  "time" TEXT /* Time of the observation */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature in Celsius */,
  "feels_like" REAL /* Feels like temperature in Celsius */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Humidity percentage */,
  "wind_direction" TEXT /* Wind direction */,
  "wind_speed" REAL /* Wind speed in km/h */,
  "wind_gust" REAL /* Wind gust speed in km/h */,
  "pressure" REAL /* Pressure in hPa */,
  "rainfall" REAL /* Rainfall in mm */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Quality of the data */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data */,
  "weather_condition" TEXT /* Weather condition at the time of observation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "historical_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the weather station collecting the data */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the observation */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature in Celsius */,
  "feels_like" REAL /* Feels like temperature in Celsius */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Humidity percentage */,
  "wind_direction" TEXT /* Wind direction */,
  "wind_speed" REAL /* Wind speed in km/h */,
  "wind_gust" REAL /* Wind gust speed in km/h */,
  "pressure" REAL /* Pressure in hPa */,
  "rainfall" REAL /* Rainfall in mm */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Quality of the data */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data */,
  "weather_condition" TEXT /* Weather condition at the time of observation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "forecasting_models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forecasting model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the forecasting model */,
  "model_description" TEXT /* Description of the forecasting model */,
  "model_accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the forecasting model */,
  "model_complexity" TEXT /* Complexity of the forecasting model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "forecast_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the forecasting model used */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the weather station */,
  "forecast_date" TEXT /* Date of the forecast */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Forecasted temperature in Celsius */,
  "feels_like" REAL /* Forecasted feels like temperature in Celsius */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Forecasted humidity percentage */,
  "wind_direction" TEXT /* Forecasted wind direction */,
  "wind_speed" REAL /* Forecasted wind speed in km/h */,
  "wind_gust" REAL /* Forecasted wind gust speed in km/h */,
  "pressure" REAL /* Forecasted pressure in hPa */,
  "rainfall" REAL /* Forecasted rainfall in mm */,
  "forecast_accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the forecast */,
  "forecast_confidence" TEXT /* Confidence in the forecast */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecast_data_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecast_data_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES forecasting_models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_warnings" (
  "warning_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather warning */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the weather station */,
  "warning_date" TEXT /* Date of the warning */,
  "warning_message" TEXT /* Text of the warning message */,
  "warning_level" TEXT /* Level of the warning */,
  "warning_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the warning */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("warning_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_warnings_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user */,
  "user_password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "user_phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access */,
  "access_location" TEXT /* Location from where the data was accessed */,
  "access_device" TEXT /* Device used to access the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES weather_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_visualizations" (
  "visualization_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data visualization */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data being visualized */,
  "visualization_type" TEXT /* Type of data visualization */,
  "visualization_data" TEXT /* Data for the visualization */,
  "visualization_format" TEXT /* Format of the visualization */,
  "visualization_size" INTEGER /* Size of the visualization */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("visualization_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_visualizations_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES weather_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_station_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status update */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather station */,
  "status_date" TEXT /* Date of the status update */,
  "status_message" TEXT /* Text of the status message */,
  "status_code" TEXT /* Status code of the weather station */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_station_status_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_station_maintenance" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance record */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather station */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the maintenance */,
  "maintenance_details" TEXT /* Details of the maintenance */,
  "maintenance_type" TEXT /* Type of maintenance */,
  "maintenance_cost" REAL /* Cost of maintenance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_station_maintenance_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_station_calibration" (
  "calibration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each calibration record */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather station */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date of the calibration */,
  "calibration_details" TEXT /* Details of the calibration */,
  "calibration_type" TEXT /* Type of calibration */,
  "calibration_result" TEXT /* Result of the calibration */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("calibration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_station_calibration_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id")
)
